items.title: Enhance Production Safety Measures in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update disables the activation of the aichat2 browser facade and the Browser Relay tool in production environments. Key observability features such as pairing, authentication, and health checks are preserved for internal readiness. The changes ensure that execution remains gated until all necessary conditions are met for safe operation.

items.title: Enhance Fish TTS Pricing Display in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the Fish TTS pricing table by explicitly displaying the 's2-pro' model instead of a placeholder. The catch-all rule is now hidden, ensuring only relevant models are shown while maintaining billing accuracy. The changes have been validated with successful tests, enhancing clarity for users.

items.title: Enhance Health Endpoints with Contract Digests in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update exposes the `wire_contract_digest` and `facade_catalog_digest` in the aichat2 `/health` endpoint, as well as the `wire_contract_digest` in Browser Relay readiness responses. Focused tests have been added to ensure the integrity of these runtime response contracts. Overall, the health status codes and dependency readiness semantics remain unchanged.

items.title: Enhance Health Endpoint with Browser Contract in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new public `/health` JSON endpoint that is dependency-free and exposes the Browser Device Bridge's `wire_contract_digest`. The existing plain-text root probe functionality remains intact and has been regression-tested to ensure stability. Overall, this enhancement improves the monitoring capabilities of the AuthBackend.

items.title: Fix grsai balance retrieval for accurate reporting in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where the grsai balance monitor incorrectly displayed a balance of ¥0. The function now correctly retrieves the account's real spendable balance using the `getCredits` API, ensuring accurate balance reporting and triggering alarms for low balances as intended.

items.title: fix discord skill frontmatter parsing issue in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical YAML parsing issue in the `discord` skill's frontmatter, which was causing the skill to be silently skipped during synchronization. The problematic colon in the description was removed, allowing the frontmatter to parse correctly and ensuring that the latest content reaches the database. This fix prevents the old read-only discord skill from being served and enhances the overall functionality of the integration.

items.title: Enhance CLI with Latest Fish API Updates in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the CLI options for the `fish` command with the latest OpenAPI specifications. Key changes include the addition of new TTS request options, improved validation for JSON inputs, and updated model query filters. These enhancements ensure better compatibility and user experience when interacting with the Fish API.

items.title: Exciting Seedance 2.0 Video Deployment in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Kubernetes deployment configuration for the Seedance 2.0 video handler, enhancing the platform's capabilities. Key additions include a new deployment and service YAML, alongside modifications to the README and deployment scripts. The deployment has been successfully verified through Kong, ensuring seamless routing and functionality.

items.title: Switch to Hosted Card Payments for Enhanced Security in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update transitions card payments from an embedded form to a hosted redirect, aligning with the US Stripe implementation. The embedded card component has been removed, and the Stripe payment component now handles both Stripe and card payment methods. This change enhances security and simplifies the payment process for users.

items.title: Enhance HK PaymentLink for Card Orders in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the payment process for Card orders in Hong Kong with the existing US Stripe flow, utilizing hosted PaymentLinks and native invoice emails. Key changes include the addition of an optional account parameter in the payment creation function, modifications to the order view for Card payments, and the implementation of dual-key verification for invoice webhooks. These enhancements ensure a consistent user experience and improved invoice handling across regions.

items.title: Enhance Stripe Support for HK Accounts and One-Time Payment Links in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for a second Stripe account in Hong Kong, allowing for one-time payment links that expire after a single use. Key changes include the addition of a new route for the HK account and modifications to ensure that payment links cannot be reused. The implementation maintains compatibility with existing US payment processes and enhances the overall payment experience.

items.title: Revamp Fish TTS Pricing with New Models in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new pricing structure for Fish TTS, offering a 30% discount on the official rate, now set at $10.50 per 1M UTF-8 bytes for models s1, s2-pro, and the newly added s2.1-pro. Key changes include updated API rules, service documentation, and tests to ensure accurate billing per model. The pricing page has been enhanced to reflect these updates, providing clear visibility of the new rates.

items.title: Enhance Fish TTS with Per-Model Billing and New Model in Platform Service 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces per-model billing for Fish TTS, allowing for separate pricing of the new s2.1-pro model alongside existing models. Key changes include updates to the getRecordMetadata function to report the resolved model and adjustments to the VALID_MODELS array. The new pricing structure is set to 30% below retail, ensuring accurate billing for all models.

items.title: Fix credential handling for Browser Relay in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update injects the `AUTH_BACKEND_RELAY_TOKEN` from the pre-provisioned Browser Relay Secret, ensuring that deployments fail if the token is absent. The changes align the Compose, environment examples, and deployment documentation with the new credential management. As a result, Browser Relay now operates with 2/2 updated replicas and zero restarts.

items.title: Enhance Browser Relay Security in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update injects the `BROWSER_RELAY_INTERNAL_TOKEN` into AuthBackend from the vault, ensuring both browser relay credentials are persisted securely. The deployment script has been made POSIX-compatible, addressing CI invocation issues, and preflight checks have been implemented to prevent partial rollouts. Overall, these changes enhance the reliability and security of the Browser Relay rollout.

items.title: Enhance Seedance with Talking Mode in Nexior 🎤
items.summary: This update introduces a default model switch to Seedance 2.0 Fast and adds a Talking Mode toggle, allowing characters to speak lines based on user prompts. The changes include automatic remapping of uploaded images to reference images, forced audio generation, and smart prompts to guide users in dialogue phrasing. Additionally, internationalization support has been expanded across multiple languages.

items.title: Streamline WeChat Setup with One-Command Flow in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update simplifies the WeChat setup process by replacing the previous multi-step command with a single command flow. Users can now enable the WeChat channel without manual edits or interactive prompts, enhancing the overall user experience. Additionally, Windows PowerShell users receive specific instructions to avoid potential issues.

items.title: Streamline WeChat Setup with One Command in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `coding-bridge channels enable wechat` command, simplifying the WeChat gateway setup process by eliminating the need for manual configuration. It automatically generates a valid `channels.toml` file, ensuring proper validation and idempotency. The changes enhance user experience by allowing a no-portal setup and preserving existing configurations during updates.

items.title: Introducing Robinhood Chain as a New Payment Network in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates the Robinhood Chain (chainId 4663) as an exact-scheme payment network for x402 settlements, utilizing USDG as the stablecoin. Key changes include the addition of configuration settings, updates to the rail policy, and dynamic currency support. The feature is defaulted to off, requiring a verified RPC URL and signer to activate.

items.title: Add per-card delete button for GPT-image and Nano Banana history in Nexior 🗑️
items.summary: This update introduces a per-card delete button for GPT-image and Nano Banana history, allowing users to remove tasks directly from the UI. The implementation includes server-side hard delete functionality and updates to the task preview components. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for multiple languages, enhancing accessibility for users worldwide.

items.title: Align Download & View-Code Buttons in Nexior 🛠️
items.summary: This update fixes the vertical misalignment of the download and view-code buttons in the Fish TTS task card. By converting the operations row to a flex layout and adjusting the button classes, the buttons are now properly aligned, matching the conventions used in other task cards. The change is scoped to the Fish TTS card, ensuring no impact on other components.

items.title: Enhance Grsai Credit Monitoring in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces Grsai to the upstream balance monitoring system, ensuring visibility for credit balances. The implementation includes a new function to query Grsai's API for credits, updates to the deployment configuration, and an alarm for low balances below ¥20. These changes will prevent silent failures in generations due to insufficient credits.

items.title: Enhance Discord Connector with BYOC User-Token Method in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new BYOC user-token method for the Discord connector, allowing users to send messages as their own account. It complements the existing OAuth method, enabling full channel actions with the appropriate credentials. Additionally, the connector's documentation has been refreshed to reflect these changes and a new messaging tag has been added.

items.title: Enhance Discord Skill with Self-Bot Capabilities in Skills 🚀
items.summary: The Discord skill now integrates both OAuth and BYOC modes, allowing users to perform full personal-account actions with their own tokens. The previous standalone `discord-user` skill has been merged into this unified skill, simplifying usage and enhancing functionality. Key updates include a new script for user actions and improved error handling during connection methods.

items.title: Enhance Chat Metadata Preservation in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the preservation of owner-bound upload metadata during chat attachment submissions in Nexior. Key changes include the addition of file_id, SHA-256, MIME type, and size to the upload response, ensuring backward compatibility with older deployments. The metadata is now structured within user message blocks, enhancing the overall chat experience.

items.title: Enhance Chat Attachment Uploads in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update completes the staging process for owner-uploaded chat attachments on the aichat-side. Key changes include validation of file UUIDs, SHA-256 checks, and MIME types, ensuring robust handling of structured references while maintaining legacy URL support. The rollout will keep the browser execution feature off until all dependencies are in place and E2E tests pass successfully.

items.title: Enhance File Uploads with Owner Metadata in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces owner-bound metadata receipts to the file upload process, enhancing the existing API without disrupting legacy responses. Key features include the addition of file identification, MIME type, and size, along with a 24-hour expiration for owner metadata. The changes ensure compatibility with existing systems while improving the management of uploaded files.

items.title: Updated Browser Device Bridge Audit for Clarity in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update refreshes the Browser Device Bridge implementation-alignment audit, ensuring accurate reporting of resource and fixture statuses. Key changes include the replacement of outdated statements with precise PR validation statuses and a comprehensive merge and deployment DAG. The rollout remains blocked pending further validations and coverage improvements.

items.title: Enhance Relay Extension Testing in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces an invalid fixture with a real execution test for the Relay Extension. Key improvements include a test-only MV3 service worker, enhanced validation processes, and fixes for startup bugs. The CI pipeline now supports headed validation using Playwright, ensuring a robust testing environment.

items.title: Preinstall discord.py-self for Discord User Skill in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update preinstalls `discord.py-self` version 2.1.0 in the sandbox image, enabling the new `discord-user` skill to operate a personal Discord account. The Dockerfile has been modified to include `discord` in the Python smoke check to ensure that any issues with the library are caught before production. This change is essential for the skill's functionality, as it cannot install dependencies at runtime.

items.title: Exciting New Seedance 2.0 Video Generation Feature in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces Seedance 2.0 video generation to the BananaRouter PlatformService, enabling users to create videos via the new `POST /seedance/videos` endpoint. The implementation supports both async and sync modes, allowing for flexible video processing and asset enrollment. Comprehensive tests have been added to ensure functionality and reliability.

items.title: Enhance Browser Resource Transfers in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the BrowserResource and BrowserArtifact transport mechanisms, enhancing the final Browser Device Bridge. Key improvements include command-bound resource resolution, metadata validation, and robust error handling, ensuring secure and efficient data transfers. Additionally, several tests have been added to validate the new functionalities.

items.title: Integrate Command Artifacts into Chat in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update merges Browser Relay `artifact_refs` into the standard aichat2 tool artifacts after command completion or recovery. Key changes include validation of artifact details and emission of standard artifacts for the chat pipeline, while capping the number of artifacts at 20. The implementation ensures robust artifact ownership verification and maintains clean execution binding.

items.title: Enhance Browser Transfers with New Receipt Layer in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive receipt layer for BrowserResource and BrowserArtifact transfers, enhancing security and user management. Key features include command-grant-bound receipts, per-user quotas, and short-lived presigned URLs, all backed by PostgreSQL. The rollout will be controlled until dependent components are fully integrated and validated.

items.title: Streamline Browser Agent Execution Path in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the obsolete synthetic Browser Agent execution path following the integration of the single-extension CDP engine. Key files such as `pageTree.ts` and `readPage.ts` have been deleted, along with the legacy test suite, ensuring a cleaner and more efficient codebase. The canonical Browser Agent now operates exclusively through updated components, maintaining existing cookie bridge behavior.

items.title: Enhance Resource Staging Grants for Browsers in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new short-lived internal `resource_prepare` authority grant for staging BrowserResources uploaded by owners. Key changes include binding user and session details, signing various metadata, and ensuring the grant is validated at verification time. This enhancement is crucial for the upcoming staged file upload feature in the PlatformBackend.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI Image Support with New Adapter in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an OpenAI-compatible image adapter for the grsai nano-banana channel, adding endpoints for image generations and edits. The integration allows for independent async 4K image processing, enhancing the platform's capabilities while ensuring billing accuracy. Key improvements include robust error handling and optimized request processing for multiple images.

items.title: Launch Production Runtime for X402 in X402 Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update promotes the X402Backend to a fail-closed production resource server, incorporating PostgreSQL for payment durability and enhanced security features like Tencent KMS. Key changes include the integration of signed quotes from PlatformBackend and the introduction of immutable Docker/Kubernetes resources. The deployment process is now staged in Hong Kong, ensuring a robust and secure environment for operations.

items.title: Exciting New Features for Signed X402 Contracts in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a fully signed x402 resource catalog, enhancing internal authentication and pricing calculations. Key improvements include the addition of idempotent usage endpoints and the integration of independent catalog and quote secrets into production. The rollout is designed to maintain existing public routing while providing robust internal functionalities.

items.title: Enhance tool call handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update finalizes the handling of assistant prose before tool calls by flushing the `ThinkTagSplitter` at the terminal `finish_reason`. Key improvements include maintaining early telemetry for tool use and adding regression tests to ensure proper splitting of text. The changes enhance the user experience by ensuring live progress is displayed accurately during tool interactions.

items.title: Enhance Live Manifest Generation in X402 Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new auditable `x402-live-manifest` generator that integrates the frozen 53-case profile with a real provider rail, enhancing the signing of EIP-3009 payloads using the official x402 Python SDK. Additionally, it includes a raw provider profile for runtime-only settlement authentication and normalizes token-domain values in calibration checks. The changes aim to improve conformance tooling without affecting production capabilities.

items.title: Increase doctor probe timeout to prevent false negatives in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: The timeout for the `channels doctor` probe has been increased from 5 seconds to 15 seconds. This change addresses issues where the probe reported connectivity problems due to cold TLS handshakes taking longer than the previous timeout. With this fix, users will no longer encounter misleading error messages during initial setup.

items.title: Fix WeChat AI Setup Snippet for Smooth Deployment in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the WeChat AI agent setup snippet in the appliance console, ensuring users can properly install CodingBridge via pipx. Key changes include the injection of the real base_url, an optional ANTHROPIC block for API key usage, and the addition of a channels smoke test for verification. These adjustments enhance the setup process and prevent common errors related to outdated installations.

items.title: Standardize Balance Terminology in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update unifies the terminology for 'Dedicated Balance' across multiple client documentation. The phrase '所属应用余额充足' has been changed to '所属应用的余额充足' in six documents to prevent confusion with the '专属余额' label. This is a purely textual change affecting only the Chinese source files.

items.title: Unify Dedicated Balance Terminology in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update standardizes the terminology for 'Dedicated Balance' across all supported languages in the Nexior application. The changes include updates to the `title.applicationBalance` and `field.remainingAmount` keys, ensuring consistency in user messaging. Additionally, a bug related to the global balance description has been fixed, enhancing clarity for users.

items.title: Unifying Dedicated Balance Terminology in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This PR standardizes the terminology for 'Dedicated Balance' across the platform, ensuring consistency in multiple languages. Key changes include renaming 'instanceBalance' to 'Dedicated Balance' and correcting related terms in various locales. The adjustments enhance clarity for users interacting with the balance features.

items.title: Fix Fish TTS Billing by Reporting Byte Count in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the billing issue with Fish TTS by ensuring that the byte count of the input text is reported correctly. The `getRecordMetadata()` method in the `FishTtsHandler` class has been overridden to return both character and byte counts, allowing the billing system to charge accurately based on usage. No changes to pricing have been made, and existing tests confirm that the per-byte pricing remains intact.

items.title: Enhance Tool Progress Handling in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of tool progress in the Nexior application by separating early tool lifecycle UI from the canonical assistant message content. It ensures that in-progress tools remain visible without splitting words and introduces a new `tool_use_commit` event for finalizing tools, enhancing compatibility with older workers. The changes have been validated with extensive testing and are ready for deployment.

items.title: Enhance Tool Call Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refines how tool calls are processed by treating early tool use events as temporary telemetry rather than final content boundaries. It ensures that tool blocks are only persisted upon complete input, preventing issues with incomplete or aborted calls. The changes improve the overall reliability of tool execution and feedback mechanisms.

items.title: Restore Default Audio Format for Fish TTS in Platform Service 🎶
items.summary: This update re-applies the default audio format for the Fish TTS service, correcting a regression that caused HTTP 400 errors due to missing format specifications. Key changes include restoring the default format to 'mp3' in settings and ensuring that the payload defaults to 'mp3' when the format is falsy. Additionally, new tests have been added to verify the functionality and prevent future issues.

items.title: Boosting 4K Resilience with Grsai Channel in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces Grsai as a new independent nano-banana image channel, enhancing the failover-router's capability to handle 2K/4K redundancy. By utilizing Grsai's async API, the system can now maintain functionality even when existing scrapers are down, significantly improving reliability. Key changes include the addition of a new worker and updates to the failover-router configuration.

items.title: Enhance Voice Picker with Public Library Search in Nexior 🎤
items.summary: The Voice Picker component now allows users to search Fish's extensive public voice library alongside their own cloned voices. Key updates include grouped sections for 'My voices' and 'Public voice library', debounced remote search functionality, and inline audio previews for each option. This enhancement significantly improves user experience by providing access to over 2.4 million voices.

items.title: Fix chat tool-call rows spinning forever in Nexior 🔄
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where tool-call rows in the chat interface would display a spinning icon indefinitely. The fix involves cloning each content part during the rendering process to ensure that Vue detects changes and updates the UI correctly. This change enhances the responsiveness of the chat tool, providing users with accurate status indicators for their tool calls.

items.title: Enhanced Credential Filter with Grouping and Masked Values in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update refines the credential filter by implementing a grouped dropdown that displays masked token values alongside their names, improving usability. Additionally, the filter row has been narrowed to ensure the Export CSV option is back on the first line, enhancing the overall layout. These changes aim to provide a more intuitive experience for users interacting with the consumption page.

items.title: Enhance x402 Delete Action for Free Access in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update allows x402 callers to delete their own generation-history records without facing a payment challenge. The change adds 'delete' to the free-action allowlist for the `/tasks` API, ensuring that users can perform this action seamlessly. Security measures remain intact, as ownership checks are enforced downstream.

items.title: New Embedded Card Payment Option in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces an embedded CreditCard payment option on the authenticated order page, utilizing Stripe's Payment Element for seamless transactions on our domain. Key changes include the addition of a new `CreditCardPay.vue` component, enhanced internationalization support, and safety measures for payment confirmation. The feature is gated by specific flags to ensure proper functionality and security.

items.title: Add embedded Card payment option for HK in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `OrderPayWay.CREDIT_CARD`, enabling an embedded Stripe Payment Element checkout for our second HK Stripe account. The existing US payment flow remains unaffected, ensuring zero risk. Key changes include the addition of a new migration for payment options and server-side handling of payment intent success events.

items.title: Add HK Stripe Account for Credit Card Flow in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a second Stripe account for Hong Kong, specifically for the new embedded Credit Card payment method, while retaining the existing US account as the default. Key changes include per-request API key handling and a new account selector for payment intents, ensuring that the HK account operates independently without falling back to the US key. Additionally, necessary deployment configurations have been updated to support this new setup.

items.title: Empower Users with Task Deletion Feature in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'delete' action for users to hard-delete their own generation-history tasks across multiple Node and Python workers. The implementation ensures robust security measures to prevent unauthorized deletions, requiring both task ID and user ID to be non-empty strings. This backend capability sets the stage for a forthcoming UI button in Nexior, enhancing user control over sensitive records.

items.title: Audit Implementation Alignment for Browser Device Bridge in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update includes a comprehensive audit of the Browser Device Bridge implementation against the authoritative plan from September to mid-October. Key blockers have been identified, including conflicts with multiple PRs and incomplete features that prevent merging or enabling the current implementation. The audit clarifies the status of various components and outlines the necessary steps to resolve outstanding issues.

items.title: Update Midjourney CLI Response Fields to Match OpenAPI Spec in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the Midjourney CLI's output for the `shorten` and `translate` commands by aligning the response field names with the OpenAPI specification. The changes include renaming `shortened_prompts` to `prompts` and `translated_content` to `content` in the output display. Additionally, mock fixtures in the tests have been updated to reflect these changes, ensuring consistency across the application.

items.title: Enhance Usage Page with Credential Filter in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new multi-select dropdown for filtering API usage by specific credentials on the Usage page of the developer console. Users can now select their credentials to view detailed consumption data, ensuring consistency across lists, charts, and CSV exports. The implementation also includes necessary internationalization updates and backend adjustments to support the new filtering feature.

items.title: Enhance API Usage Filtering by Token in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new filtering capability for API usage by specific credential IDs, allowing users to view usage data for individual tokens. Four API endpoints have been modified to support the `?credential_id=` parameter, enhancing the granularity of data retrieval without compromising security. Additionally, new unit tests have been added to ensure the functionality works as intended.

items.title: Final Browser Device Engine Integrated in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates the final Browser Device engine into the PlatformFrontend extension, enhancing capabilities with mandatory CDP/session/command features. Key changes include the implementation of a two-stage Relay owner proof and a durable local ledger, while removing legacy paths. Extensive validation has been completed with successful TypeScript checks and tests.

items.title: Upgrade Xiaohongshu to Browser Command V2 in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update pins Xiaohongshu to the Browser command V2 manifest and replaces 17 authorization entries with a more stable policy class. It retains all ergonomic mappings while ensuring that routes and workflows remain within the Skill layer. Validation tests have confirmed the successful implementation of these changes.

items.title: Enhance Browser Execution Experience in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces prototype browser states with a finalized session lifecycle and typed recovery actions. Key improvements include binding scheduled browser execution to specific connection revisions and surfacing session provenance on artifacts. Additionally, the fallback for Desktop local execution has been removed, streamlining the user experience.

items.title: Finalizing Aichat2 Execution Features in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update implements key features for the Aichat2 execution, including the exposure of browser schemas and enhanced session management. It introduces new tools for consent receipt handling and removes legacy components, ensuring a more streamlined browser interaction. Comprehensive tests have validated the changes, paving the way for improved functionality.

items.title: Enhance Device Management UI for Browsers in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update finalizes the device management UI for browsers, incorporating the complete BrowserDevice summary contract. Key improvements include enhanced connection details, better handling of revoked devices, and localization support across 17 languages. The UI now provides a more intuitive experience for managing active sessions and device compatibility.

items.title: Enhance Browser Authority Grants in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the generic browser-grant surface with a more robust authority system, including session management and immutable receipts. Key features include enhanced security measures, such as pre-session signed attestations and device/account tombstones, ensuring a more reliable and secure user experience. The changes also streamline the authorization process and improve the overall architecture of the AuthBackend.

items.title: Migrated Examples to Official v2 Wire in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update successfully migrates all public examples and E2E scenario scripts from the legacy `X-Payment` to the official `PAYMENT-SIGNATURE`. Key changes include the introduction of canonical CAIP-2 network IDs and the v2 `amount` field, along with a static guard to prevent legacy wire examples from being returned. Compatibility with the production Gateway remains intact, ensuring external clients can still utilize the legacy system while transitioning to the new standard.

items.title: Cleanup of Disabled X402 Bulk Scripts in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the permanently disabled x402b Base, Solana, and swarm synthetic-payment scripts, along with their associated CronJobs. Additionally, stale references to these scripts in Tencent Cloud credential documentation have been eliminated. The cleanup enhances the overall codebase by removing unnecessary components and ensuring no production references remain.

items.title: Retired facilitator2 canary workflow in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the retired facilitator2 canary workflow along with all associated deploy and canary manifests. The README and migration documentation have been updated to reflect the successful cutover to the official-v2 production. All production deployment and rollback constraints remain intact, ensuring a smooth transition.

items.title: Enhance Browser Relay Sessions in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a two-stage device-signed owner proof for Relay sessions, enhancing security and session management. Key changes include the introduction of explicit session lifecycle events, Redis queue delivery for session events, and improved validation for session grants and time attestations. These enhancements aim to optimize the browser Relay functionality and ensure robust session handling.

items.title: Enhance Integration Guide Panel for Theme Consistency in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: The Integration guide panel on the API docs page has been updated to align with the light/dark theme of the rest of the documentation. Key changes include the use of Element Plus theme variables for the panel's styling, ensuring a cohesive look and improved readability. The embedded code terminal remains dark by design, preserving its intended aesthetic.

items.title: Enhance Release Closure with Browser Device Bridge Digest Gate in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a Browser Device Bridge digest gate to the release closure process, ensuring that all components agree on a single canonical manifest before enabling the feature flag. Key checks include verifying the `wire_contract_digest` and `facade_catalog_digest` across various components. This change aims to maintain compatibility and prevent issues during releases involving the Browser Device Bridge.

items.title: Enhance CI with Separate Concurrency Groups for Mobile Builds in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces distinct concurrency groups for iOS and Android mobile builds, preventing testing and production workflows from canceling each other. By appending production-specific inputs to the concurrency group keys, we ensure that both testing and production builds can run without interference. The daily auto-submit functionality remains unaffected and continues to operate smoothly.

items.title: Enhance Device Identity Locking Mechanism in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update implements the authoritative Browser Device Bridge plan, enhancing the locking mechanism for device contracts. Key changes include the introduction of a new wire identity for BrowserDevice and PairingClaim, as well as strict validation processes for device transactions. The migration ensures no compatibility issues with existing models, and extensive testing confirms the robustness of the new features.

items.title: Enhance Browser Device Bridge Contract in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new Browser Device Bridge contract, replacing the legacy implementation with a clean, authoritative version. Key features include strict session management, detailed command/result schemas, and comprehensive validation through multiple testing frameworks. The changes ensure improved reliability and security for browser interactions within the Platform Service.

items.title: Revamp Project Start with Guided Overview in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a guided project start for Poivelle, enhancing user experience by replacing the initial screen with a structured overview of the production workflow. Key features include explicit action prompts, maximum cost confirmations, and a requirement for frame selection before proceeding. The changes aim to streamline the project initiation process and improve usability across devices.

items.title: Optimize CI for Nexior Builds in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update significantly reduces GitHub Actions costs by changing the scheduling of macOS builds from per-merge to a daily schedule. Key improvements include implementing a CocoaPods cache to save time and resources, and adding concurrency controls to cancel in-progress builds. These changes aim to maintain efficiency while minimizing expenses associated with macOS builds.

items.title: Unify Admin Nav Group Header Style in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update standardizes the font weight and color of the Admin collapsible group header in the console sidebar to match its sibling navigation rows. The changes ensure a consistent appearance across the interface, enhancing user experience without affecting functionality. The chevron remains to indicate the collapsible nature of the group.

items.title: Enhanced DM Support for Discord Bot in Skills 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces a controlled `send-opt-in-dm` helper for the Discord Bot, ensuring that DMs are sent only after verifying the source message, author, and recipient. It includes a dry-run feature and requires confirmation to prevent accidental messages. Additionally, the implementation sanitizes API errors and suppresses duplicate messages, enhancing overall reliability.

items.title: Enhance Discord DM Support in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces permission-first single-recipient DM support for the Discord Bot connector. It requires an advertised keyword along with recipient and message confirmations, while maintaining the existing bot-token and authorization contracts. Key tests have been added to ensure functionality and compliance with the new features.

items.title: Update custom-auth plugin to 0.13.0 in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the TSE Kong `custom-auth` plugin from version `0.12.0` to `0.13.0`, with a focus on forwarding `PAYMENT-SIGNATURE`. The behavior of `access.lua` and `schema.lua` remains unchanged, ensuring compatibility. All tests have passed, confirming the integrity of the release.

items.title: Patch high-severity vulnerabilities in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses two high-severity transitive dev-dependency vulnerabilities in the Extension CI job. The `brace-expansion` and `fast-uri` packages have been updated to secure versions, ensuring that the project passes all validation checks with zero vulnerabilities. No changes were made to the source or package manifest files.

items.title: Align Status Tags and Actions for Consistency in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update aligns the status tags and adjacent action buttons using Element Plus's native dimensions, ensuring a consistent look across various components. Key changes include adjustments in the order detail, order list, appliance subscription bar, and site domain header. A focused contract has been added to verify the installed Element Plus CSS and the correct pairing of tags and buttons.

items.title: Streamline Skill Installation Process in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the pinning of skills to specific tags, allowing for the installation of the latest version directly from the default branch. Key changes include modifications to installation templates, source links, and validation processes, ensuring a smoother and more up-to-date user experience. All tests have passed successfully, confirming the effectiveness of these changes.

items.title: Fix X402 Order Replay Issues in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves two critical E2E regressions related to X402 order replay functionality. Authenticated owners can now successfully replay Finished/Paid X402 orders and receive cached payment responses without initiating a new settlement. Additionally, settlement receipts are now correctly persisted under the appropriate metadata structure, ensuring compatibility with legacy order data.

items.title: Automate HK Cluster Deployments in Germey Tech 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an automated deployment workflow for the HK cluster, triggered by pushes to the main branch. The new GitHub Actions workflow installs kubectl, loads the kubeconfig from a secure secret, and executes the existing deployment script. This streamlines the deployment process, ensuring efficient updates without manual intervention.

items.title: Updated Footer Copyright for a Cleaner Look in Germey Tech ✨
items.summary: The footer copyright has been updated across all six pages to remove 'LLC', simplifying the text to '© 2026 Germey Technology. All rights reserved.' This change enhances the visual clarity of the site while maintaining the formal legal name in the contact section. The update was a straightforward text edit with no impact on functionality.

items.title: Fix Wisdom Controls and Appliance Presets Rendering in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves issues with the Wisdom deployment page, ensuring all necessary Element Plus components are properly imported and registered. It also normalizes the schema for resource presets, allowing for a seamless user experience when selecting deployment options. Additionally, the UI has been enhanced for better accessibility and visual clarity.

items.title: Fix TTS Task Error Display in Nexior 🎤
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where failed Fish Audio TTS tasks were incorrectly displayed as pending forever. The failure detection logic has been enhanced to recognize errors based on the response status and message, ensuring users receive clear feedback on task failures. Additionally, a new utility function has been introduced to convert raw error messages into user-friendly formats.

items.title: Updated API Documentation for Latest Models in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update refreshes the API documentation to reflect the latest flagship models across various families, including OpenAI's GPT-5.6, Grok-4.5, Gemini-3.5-flash, GLM-5.2, and Claude-Fable-5. Key changes include updated keywords, overviews, and examples to ensure consistency with the live platform's source of truth. Historical response snapshots remain intact, preserving the integrity of recorded data.

items.title: Updated flagship models in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the `ai-chat` skill's model table by adding the latest flagship chat models from OpenAI, Grok, and Gemini. The models `gpt-5.6-luna`, `gpt-5.6-terra`, `gpt-5.6-sol`, `grok-4.5`, and `gemini-3.5-flash` are now prioritized, ensuring alignment with the platform's source of truth. The changes are automatically propagated through symlinks, maintaining consistency across the documentation.

items.title: Enhance AiChat with New Flagship Models in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces several flagship chat models to the AiChat MCP, including gpt-5.6, grok-4.5, gemini-3.5-flash, and glm-5.2. These additions align the MCP with the latest platform configurations, ensuring that the AiChatV2Model can accept these models for improved functionality. A new contract test has also been implemented to verify the integrity of these changes.

items.title: Enhance x402 Payment Handling in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for parsing base64 `PAYMENT-REQUIRED` headers across TypeScript, Python, and Go. It ensures that payment handlers receive the necessary canonical requirements and implements robust handling for empty 402 bodies and malformed headers. Additionally, the Go streaming requests now support x402 payment retries, enhancing the overall payment processing capabilities.

items.title: Enhance Payment Handling with Official x402 v2 Transport Lane in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the official x402 v2 transport lane, enabling the emission of standard `PAYMENT-REQUIRED` challenges and accepting `PAYMENT-SIGNATURE` for routing to the formal Facilitator. Legacy `X-Payment` handling is preserved for rollback, while improvements in observability and credential limit annotations for Python 3.9 are included. The changes have been validated with all tests passing successfully.

items.title: Upgrade to Official X402 v2 for Wallet Payments in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update migrates wallet payment processing to the official `@acedatacloud/x402-client@2026.708.0`, enhancing order signature handling for EVM and Solana. Key changes include replacing the legacy `X-PAYMENT` header with `PAYMENT-SIGNATURE`, improved wallet selection for various networks, and the removal of outdated Solana helpers. The changes ensure a more secure and standardized payment process across platforms.

items.title: Enhanced Payment Migration to X402 v2 in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update successfully migrates console order payments from X402 v1 to the official X402 2.16.0 version. Key changes include the introduction of canonical CAIP-2 challenges, improved authentication for settlement, and the requirement of a transaction identifier before crediting orders. The deployment ensures that production networks are pinned to canonical identifiers, enhancing stability and security.

items.title: Enhance Console Navigation with New Model List & Developer Docs in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two new sidebar entries in the developer console: 'Model List' and 'Developer Docs', enhancing user navigation. The sidebar has been reordered to improve accessibility, placing these new links prominently for easier access. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for these new entries across multiple languages.
